notes: Floor empirically set to 0.32. PCA transformation creates non-monotonic feature effects: minimal patients (0 procedures, 0 labs) score ~0.44, while well-managed patients (routine procedures, labs, controlled glucose) score ~0.27. Low-risk presets use the latter profile.

high_risk.rationale: High-risk profile motivated by top drivers: elevated emergency visits (SHAP +0.0335), inpatient admits (SHAP +0.0304), higher diagnosis burden (SHAP +0.0126). Facility discharge instead of home (SHAP-protective −0.0093).

moderate_risk.acceptance_criteria: 0.32 <= predicted_probability < 0.4556; risk_band == 'moderate'; prediction_label may be either
moderate_risk.rationale: Moderate profile balances some utilization and burden against protective factors (home discharge, fewer medications). Sits between explicit floor (0.35) and operating threshold (0.4556).

low_risk.rationale: Stable well-managed diabetic: normal A1C/glucose, steady insulin, routine procedure and labs, home discharge. PCA components associate thorough routine care (procedures, labs) with lower readmission risk, producing probabilities ~0.27.

recalibration_trigger_conditions: Deployed operating threshold changes (e.g., after model retraining)
Best model artifact replaced (deployment_pipeline.joblib hash changes)
Feature engineering or selection parameters change
Data distribution materially shifts (monitored via fairness/calibration metrics)
